metadata.description: Master dataset for SHIFT AI Employment Impact Dashboard - Saudi Arabia

sources.ai_exposure: Frey-Osborne 2017, Eloundou/OpenAI 2023, Felten/Raj/Seamans AIOE, Brynjolfsson SML, McKinsey 2024, WEF Future of Jobs 2025
sources.layoffs: Layoffs.fyi via TechCrunch, Challenger Gray & Christmas
sources.vision_2030: Official V2030 programs, sector authorities, PwC, OBG
sources.ecosystem: SDAIA, Stanford AI Index 2025, Tortoise GARI, company reports

metadata.methodology_note: AI risk scores are composite indices (0-100) calculated from academic studies. They represent EXPOSURE, not predicted job loss. Augmentation (AI as co-pilot) vs substitution (AI replaces worker) distinction is critical.

hero.methodology_note: Calculated as SUM(sector_employees × sector_ai_risk_score/100). This represents AI-weighted exposure, not predicted job losses. Actual displacement will be lower (augmentation > substitution for many roles).

sectors.ai_risk_rationale: Physical outdoor work, non-structured environments. Precision agriculture emerging but low GenAI impact. Robotics limited by terrain variability.

AI augments exploration, predictive maintenance, process optimization. But physical extraction, safety, on-site ops remain human. Aramco already heavy AI adopter for efficiency, not headcount reduction.
sectors.name_ar: التعدين واستغلال المحاجر (بما فيها النفط والغاز)

sectors.ai_risk_rationale: Robotics and automation in assembly lines. But KSA manufacturing still scaling up (NIDLP). Net job creation expected despite automation of routine tasks. Quality control AI high impact.
Smart grid management, predictive maintenance. But small sector, high Saudisation already. Renewables expansion will ADD jobs.
Physical infrastructure work. IoT monitoring augments but doesn't replace. Desalination expansion = more jobs.

Largest sector. Physical, non-structured environments. Robotics decades away from replacing manual labor on chaotic construction sites. NEOM, Red Sea, Qiddiya, Diriyah = massive ongoing demand. WEF confirms BTP workers among fastest growing.

sectors.name_fr: Commerce de gros et de détail
sectors.ai_risk_rationale: Self-checkout (Frey-Osborne 97% cashier), e-commerce replacing physical stores, automated inventory. But WEF still shows shop salespeople growing. Mixed: substitution for cashiers, augmentation for sales advisors.
Autonomous vehicles Level 5 still distant. SPL deploying 200 sorting robots. Warehouse automation rising. But delivery drivers in strong GROWTH (WEF). Long-term risk medium, short-term growth.

Hospitality requires human warmth, physical service. Self-ordering kiosks reduce some roles. But tourism boom (150M visitors target) = massive NET job creation. Foodics optimizes staffing but doesn't eliminate it.

PARADOX SECTOR. AI transforms ICT jobs (GitHub Copilot for coders) but also creates massive new roles (AI/ML specialists, data engineers). Net effect: strong growth with internal restructuring. Eloundou score 1.0 for programmers but category = augmentation, not substitution.

HIGHEST GenAI exposure. ANB automated 35 processes, 40K hours saved. Bank tellers -35% (WEF). Accounting 94% Frey-Osborne. But advisory/relationship roles grow. Small sector with very high Saudisation = mainly Saudi jobs at risk of transformation.
PropTech, AI valuation models, automated listings. But transaction complexity and trust require humans. Small sector.

Lawyers, consultants, accountants, architects - all heavily exposed to GenAI. But overwhelmingly augmentation not substitution. The accountant who uses AI replaces the one who doesn't. High-skill sector with transformation, not destruction.

sectors.name_fr: Activités de services administratifs et de soutien
sectors.ai_risk_rationale: HIGHEST RISK SECTOR BY VOLUME. Data entry (99% Frey-Osborne), admin assistants (90%+), back-office processing. 1.4M workers, 83% expat. This is where AI hits hardest: repetitive cognitive tasks at scale. BPO, facilities mgmt partially protected but office admin decimated.

Absher automated 12M+ operations. ZATCA 50% customs automated. Digital Government saving billions. But no DOGE-style mass layoffs - gradual non-replacement and upskilling. Protected by political will.

Teachers among most protected (0.74% Frey-Osborne). Emotional intelligence, character formation irreplaceable. AI augments (adaptive learning) but demand GROWS (WEF: higher education teachers top 15). Admin roles within education at risk though.

Fortress of human employment. Doctors 0.42%, nurses 0.90% Frey-Osborne. Legal liability, empathy, physical care = irreplaceable. AI as diagnostic co-pilot only. 175K additional professionals NEEDED by 2030. KFSHRC shows augmentation model.

Physical performance, live events, theme parks = human-centric. AI generative for content creation but live entertainment booming. GEA + Qiddiya + Seasons = massive growth. Graphic designers at risk but performers protected.
Mixed bag: personal services, repair, laundry. Many physical/manual tasks protected. Some admin functions at risk.
Negligible sector in GOSI data. Domestic workers largely outside GOSI coverage.

wef.headline: 22% of all jobs structurally disrupted by 2030. Net +78M globally (170M created - 92M destroyed).

ai_cases_global.reason: AI-powered ad products replacing account management
Restructuring to reallocate budget to AI engineering
ChatGPT destroying homework help demand, 45% workforce cut
Developers switching to AI coding assistants
10% contractors replaced by GPT-4 for translation
AI assistant replaced 700 FTE equivalent in customer service
10K of 55K total cuts explicitly AI/automation by 2030
Hiring freeze on AI-replaceable back-office roles over 5 years
Realignment to AI platform NEXA, 24-32% of staff
Customer service and sales admin automated
AI-powered job matching replacing manual R&D

challenger.note: AI is 2nd cause after cost-cutting in Oct 2025. Strong upward trend.

ksa_ai_cases.impact: 35 processes automated, 100+ bots, 40K hours saved, 2M+ requests processed
ksa_ai_cases.jobs_impact: Back-office data entry and compliance partially replaced

ksa_ai_cases.impact: 200 sorting robots deployed, largest robotic sorting center in Middle East
Fully automated dark stores for e-commerce fulfillment
12M+ operations in H1 2023, 17B SAR annual savings
ksa_ai_cases.jobs_impact: Government counter staff demand reduced via non-replacement
Document processing clerks replaced by AI workflows

ksa_ai_cases.impact: 20+ AI applications, 170K automated interventions, 25% diagnostic accuracy improvement
ksa_ai_cases.jobs_impact: Augmentation: radiologists freed from routine, no reported layoffs

ksa_ai_cases.impact: Native autonomous transport, predictive maintenance, AI-first urban management
ksa_ai_cases.jobs_impact: Traditional urban jobs (drivers, counter staff) structurally absent from design

ksa_ai_cases.impact: Fully cashierless store using AI vision and auto-payment
ksa_ai_cases.jobs_impact: All cashier positions eliminated in pilot store

ksa_ai_cases.impact: 800 positions cut (~10% workforce), accelerated by Covid + digitalization
Cruise and TXAI pilot programs for autonomous taxis
ksa_ai_cases.jobs_impact: Strategic direction: progressive replacement of human drivers by 2030

summary.note: Gross total has significant overlap (NIDLP includes mining+manufacturing+logistics, giga-projects overlap with tourism/construction). 30% deduplication applied = conservative net estimate.

nitaqat.overview: Nitaqat mandates minimum Saudization rates by sector and company size. Rates are increasing aggressively to 2028.
nitaqat.expat_double_risk_note: Expat workers face DOUBLE risk: AI automation score + Saudization pressure. Example: expat accountant = high AI risk (94% Frey-Osborne) + high Nitaqat risk (40%→70% by 2028).
